title: Name angles
description: Name angles by their vertex, endpoints, or labels.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a name for the marked angle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can name the angle using $3$ points, one on each side of the angle and one at the vertex.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The vertex point *must* be the middle letter.  The other letters can be in either order: ${\angle D\greenD{A}E}$ or ${\angle E\greenD{A}D}$.
A name for the angle is $\angle{EAD}$.

options.alt: Four rays. One ray starts at point A, then points to the left and slightly up through point E. A second ray starts at point A, then points up and slightly to the left through point D. A third ray starts at point A, then points up and slightly to the right through point C. A fourth ray starts at point A, then points to the right and slightly up through point B. A green curved line connects ray AE to ray AD.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a name for the marked angle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can name the angle using $3$ points, one on each side of the angle and one at the vertex.   

[[☃ image 1]]

The vertex point *must* be the middle letter.  The other letters can be in either order: ${\angle D\blueD{A}C}$ or ${\angle C\blueD{A}D}$.
A name for the angle is $\angle{CAD}$.

options.alt: Four rays. One ray starts at point A, then points to the left and slightly down through point E. A second ray starts at point A, then points down and slightly to the right through point B. A third ray starts at point A, then points down and to the right through point C. A fourth ray starts at point A, then points to the right and slightly up through point D. A blue curved line connects ray AC to ray AD.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a name for the marked angle?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can name the angle using $3$ points, one on each side of the angle and one at the vertex.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The vertex point *must* be the middle letter.  The other letters can be in either order: ${\angle B\purpleD{A}D}$ or ${\angle 
D\purpleD{A}B}$.
A name for the angle is $\angle{BAD}$.

options.alt: Three rays. One ray starts at point A, then points to the right and slightly up through point D. A second ray starts at point A, then points to the right and slightly down through point C. A third ray starts at point A, then points down and slightly to the right through point B. A purple curved line passes through ray AC and connects ray AB to ray AD.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is a name for the marked angle?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
We can name the angle using $3$ points, one on each side of the angle and one at the vertex.  

[[☃ image 1]]

The vertex point *must* be the middle letter.  The other letters can be in either order: ${\angle E\blueD{A}F}$ or ${\angle 
F\blueD{A}E}$.
A name for the angle is $\angle{EAF}$.

options.alt: Five rays. One ray starts at point A, then points down and to the left through point B. A second ray starts at point A, then points up and to the left through point E. A third ray starts at point A, then points up through point F. A fourth ray starts at point A, then points up and to the right through point D. A fifth ray starts at point A, then points down and to the right through point C.  A blue curved line connects ray AE to ray AF.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which angle refers to the same angle as $\angle BPF$?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find point $B, P$ and $F$ on the figure. 

[[☃ image 1]]

What angle contains all $3$ of those points and has $P$ as the vertex? 
$\angle BPF$ names this angle:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Four rays. One ray starts at point P, then points to the left through point A. A second ray starts at point P, then points up and to the right through point F. A third ray starts at point P, then points to the right through point B. A fourth ray starts at point P, then points down and to the left through point G. Three blue curved lines connect ray PB to ray PG. An orange curved line connects ray PA to ray PF. Two green curved lines connect ray PF to ray PB. Three blue curved lines connect ray PB to ray PG. Four purple curved lines connect ray PG to ray PA.

choices.content: ![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 1 orange curved line. One segment extends to the left from the vertex. A second segment extends up and to the right from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3623bc8137eba29010351088315a7f4b02556df)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 2 green curved lines. One segment extends up and to the right from the vertex. A second segment extends to the right from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/32c01e9bce535cb64c871fa413e5762e988afac4)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 3 blue curved lines. One segment extends to the right from the vertex. A second segment extends down and to the left from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/54b8cc94c80a8576bdb70b1bbb23347b3fc5d082)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 4 purple curved lines. One segment extends down and to the left from the vertex. A second segment extends to the left from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/835a6d24bc880ea9dbfffc1bbec42e420f58b3ca)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which angle refers to the same angle as $\angle DEF$?**  


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find point $D, E$ and $F$ on the figure. 


[[☃ image 1]]

What angle contains all $3$ of those points and has $E$ as the vertex? 
$\angle DEF$ names this angle:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A six-pointed star figure made up of 6 equal-sized diamonds. The center point is labeled with the letter O. From the center point, the 4 points of the top diamond are labeled O, B, C, and D. Moving clockwise to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, D, E, and F. Moving clockwise again to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, F, G, and H. Moving clockwise again to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, H, I, and J. Moving clockwise again to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, J, K, and L. Moving clockwise one last time to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, L, A, B. An orange curved line connects segments BA and BO. Two green curved lines connect segments ED and EF. Three blue curved lines connect segments OD and OB. Four purple curved lines connect segments FO and FG.

choices.content: ![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by a single orange curve line. One segment extends to the left from the vertex. A second segment extends down and to the right from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fcea2be4828ca0e8156731f9d47fa3e57f962155)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 2 green curved lines. One segment extends to the left from the vertex. A second segment extends down and to the left from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8f82413adf6439716a2c857a6341e2472cf81a89)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 3 blue curved lines. One segment extends up and to the left from the vertex. A second segment extends up and to the right from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ccc8e9cac543bb8a2a09acbd62b141bff26d57e2)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 4 purple curved lines. One segment extends left from the vertex. A second segment extends down and to the right from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f13d08cc12d90f3ac11a3a136276235724e86d3c)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which angle refers to the same angle as $\angle DOF$?**  

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find point $D, O$ and $F$ on the figure. 
 

[[☃ image 1]]

What angle contains all $3$ of those points and has $O$ as the vertex? 
$\angle DOF$ names this angle:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A six-pointed star figure made up of 6 equal-sized diamonds. The center point is labeled with the letter O. From the center point, the 4 points of the top diamond are labeled O, B, C, and D. Moving clockwise to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, D, E, and F. Moving clockwise again to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, F, G, and H. Moving clockwise again to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, H, I, and J. Moving clockwise again to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, J, K, and L. Moving clockwise one last time to the next diamond in the star figure, the 4 points are labeled O, L, A, B. An orange curved line connects segments OJ and OL. Two green curved lines connect segments OJ and OH. Three blue curved lines connect segments OF and OD. Four purple curved lines connect segments OD and OB.

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 2 green curved lines. One segment extends down and to the right from the vertex. A second segment extends down and to the left from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/469cf4274b415afd0f7def71b57fe13ef0956498)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 3 blue curved lines. One segment extends up and to the right from the vertex. A second segment extends to the right from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/73518dcfc3ba5aeb17ad2151ffb6f4607540e608)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 4 purple curved lines. One segment extends up and to the right from the vertex. A second segment extends up and to the left from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/52bc9274db81fa036b6e3a5d5252bae32de6b06c)

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
Let's find point $G, P$ and $B$ on the figure.  


[[☃ image 1]]

What angle contains all $3$ of those points and has $P$ as the vertex? 
$\angle GPB$ names this angle:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Four rays. One ray starts at point P, then points to the left through point A. A second ray starts at point P, then points up and to the right through point F. A third ray starts at point P, then points to the right through point B. A fourth ray starts at point P, then points down and to the left through point G. A brown curved line connects ray PA to ray PF. Two green curved lines connect ray PF to ray PB. Three blue curved lines connect ray PB to ray PG. Four purple curved lines connect ray PG to ray PA.

choices.content: ![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 1 brown curved line. One segment extends to the left from the vertex. A second segment extends up and to the right from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a3623bc8137eba29010351088315a7f4b02556df)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 3 blue curved lines. One segment extends to the right from the vertex. A second segment extends down and to the left from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a07ff7b0fb9898eef3d17fb9c5091b5eabadc628)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 4 purple curved lines. One segment extends down and to the left from the vertex. A second segment extends to the left from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4aa5ed72f079ebbe8e0b93980058e1309ab1d0f3)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which angle refers to the same angle as $\angle CPB$?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find point $C, P$ and $B$ on the figure. 

[[☃ image 1]]

What angle contains all $3$ of those points and has $P$ as the vertex? 
$\angle CPB$ names this angle:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Line ED is above line AB, both extending left to right. Line CD starts at the bottom left, with point C below line AB and just to the right of point A. It extends upwards to the right, intersecting line AB at point P and line ED at point D. A single orange curved line connects segments PA and PC. Two green curved lines connect segments DE and DP. Three blue curved lines connect segments PB and PC. Four purple curved lines connect segments PD and PB.

choices.content: ![One ray extends left from the vertex. A second ray extends downward and left from the vertex. A single orange curved line connects these rays.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/185f805836b07a4dff76d4911214cd417aba66e4)

![One ray extends left from the vertex. A second ray extends downward and left from the vertex. Two green curved lines connect these rays.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5354b363ef54a68231097a6ffad47bd6275aa80a)

![One ray extends right from the vertex. A second ray extends downward and left from the vertex. Three blue curved lines connect these rays.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/da77b4e50b05022963d9fc83961b3c864d04986c)

![One ray extends right from the vertex. A second ray extends upward and right from the vertex. Four purple curved lines connect these rays.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2a2ba7556793da97aa9fb215d3a154eea497c41e)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which angle refers to the same angle as $\angle DAC$?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Let's find point $D, A$ and $C$ on the figure.  

[[☃ image 1]]

What angle contains all $3$ of those points and has $A$ as the vertex? 
$\angle DAC$ names this angle:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Five rays. One ray starts at point A, then points to the left through point C. A second ray starts at point A, then points up and to the left through point D. A third ray starts at point A, then points up and slightly to the right through point F. A fourth ray starts at point A, then points to the right and slightly up through point E. A fifth ray starts at point A, then points to the right through point B.  A brown curved line connects ray AC to ray AD. Two green curved lines connect ray AD to ray AF. Three blue curved lines connect ray AF to ray AE. Four purple curved lines connect ray AE to ray AB.

choices.content: ![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 1 brown curved line. One segment extends to the left from the vertex. A second segment extends up and to the left from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9006dbc2f0858eb5f5f335c34bf27af9fa63d505)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 2 green curved lines. One segment extends up and to the left from the vertex. A second segment extends up and to the right from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/858d1d158e0f95a98acbfcd6cbb06da644e6ab2e)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 3 blue curved lines. One segment extends up and slightly to the right from the vertex. A second segment extends right and slightly up from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f4a30a64a150f0c18d98de6bb31053b5311cc998)

![An angle is formed by 2 segments connected by 4 purple curved lines. One segment extends up and to the right from the vertex. A second segment extends to the right from the vertex.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d534f4e4ce28394935be76c84d309ba896e7a297)
